name: Orange Mint
description: Orange Mint (Mentha x piperita f. citrata), a citrus-scented hybrid mint variety, is mildly toxic to cats. Like all Mentha species, it contains pulegone and menthol-related terpenoid compounds that cats cannot efficiently metabolise. While the orange-citrus fragrance may attract some cats, ingestion causes gastrointestinal upset.
safetyNotes: Toxic compounds: pulegone, linalool, and citrus-scented monoterpenoids; concentration increases in the flowers and essential oil
Mechanism: cats lack efficient glucuronidation for these terpenoids, leading to GI irritation and mild hepatic stress with repeated exposure
Symptoms: vomiting, diarrhea, drooling, lethargy; typically mild and self-limiting
Fresh leaf nibbling usually causes only transient mild upset; essential oil forms are far more dangerous
Keep mint plants and all mint-derived essential oils away from cats

symptoms.name: Vomiting, diarrhea, excessive drooling (foaming at the mouth), difficulty swallowing, and loss of appetite
symptoms.notes: A tiny taste of the fresh plant usually results in mild stomach upset. However, if the cat consumes larger amounts or is exposed to concentrated essential oils, the gastrointestinal distress becomes far more dangerous6. 2. Neurological Impairment

symptoms.name: Lethargy, profound weakness, uncoordinated gait (ataxia), muscle tremors, and seizures
symptoms.onset: Shortly after absorption into the bloodstream.
symptoms.notes: These severe neurological signs are most often triggered by exposure to the highly concentrated essential oils found in the plant, rather than just a casual nibble of a leaf36. 3. Respiratory Distress

symptoms.name: Difficulty breathing, coughing, and sneezing
symptoms.onset: Immediate (if oils are inhaled) to shortly after exposure
symptoms.notes: Strong mint aromas and essential oils can heavily irritate a cat's sensitive respiratory system. This can occur not only from ingestion but also from simply inhaling the diffused oils from the plant69. 4. Liver Damage / Failure

symptoms.name: Liver damage progressing to liver failure
symptoms.onset: Delayed (often developing over several hours or days as the liver struggles to metabolize the toxins
symptoms.notes: Because a cat's liver lacks the specific enzymes necessary to break down plant essential oils, the toxins build up and place dangerous, potentially fatal stress on the liver2. 5. Dermal and Mucous Membrane Burns

symptoms.name: Skin irritation, redness, or burns on the lips, gums, tongue, or skin
symptoms.notes: This localized irritation occurs when the cat's skin or mouth comes into direct physical contact with the plant's irritating oils or sap
